symbols.name: fsl_floor_minimum
symbols.provenance: Empirically discovered from 459 historical missions. Minimum Jaccard score among zero-critical-invariant PASSING missions = 5/6 exactly. Gives BASE = 1/(1 - 5/6) - 1 = 5. Structural floor formula: floor(m) = 1 - 1/(6 + n_critical_per_pkg(m)).
symbols.open_question: Is 5/6 a structural invariant of the FSL type system (determined by graph topology, independent of which missions were run), or a contingent fact about this system's history? If structural, it is a governance coupling constant. Current status: OPEN. Proof obligation at the Gödel boundary.
symbols.semantics: Minimum coherence floor for any FSL-governed mission. floor(m) >= 5/6 for all m.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: r9_surface_mandatory
symbols.semantics: R9 (rule_9) mandates that the surface registry (K_surfaces) is consistent at every mission open. Enforced by the R9 gate in open_mission() — missing surface YAML blocks the mission.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: r3_auth_required
symbols.semantics: R3 (rule_3) requires that an UpdatePacket (UP) exists before any build proceeds. log_authorization_proof() fires the R3 proof term only when a packet_id is present — no packet, no authorization.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: chain_integrity
symbols.semantics: When all stability gates pass (all_passed), a StateRuleProof (SRP) is emitted by governor_countersign(). Mission pass implies a valid, signed state proof exists in the chain.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: surface_registry_exact
symbols.semantics: A consistent surface registry (K_surfaces) implies the invariant set (K_invariants) is well-defined. The 7 canonical surfaces declared in open_guard._TRUTH_SURFACES and constitution/SYSTEM.yaml are the foundation of all runtime invariants.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: log_immutable
symbols.semantics: A LogEventPacket (LE) records state without modifying the knowledge base. mission_log.jsonl is append-only — emitting a log event does not produce a knowledge delta (ΔK). Log events observe; they do not change.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: coherence_floor
symbols.semantics: Coherence gate pass (score >= COHERENCE_FLOOR=0.75) implies mission may close with all_passed. Enforced by coherence_engine.py — gate failure blocks close_mission().
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: lipschitz_bound
symbols.semantics: Lipschitz gate pass (empirical L <= declared_blast_radius) is a precondition for the coherence gate. g_L implies g_C can be computed and holds.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: scope_declared
symbols.semantics: R6 (bounded_execution) requires a non-empty allowed_packages declaration. A declared scope is the input to the Lipschitz verifier — no scope means no g_L.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: gate_chain
symbols.semantics: Compositional gate chain: scope declaration (R6) triggers Lipschitz gate (g_L), which enables coherence gate (g_C), which gates mission pass (all_passed). All three gates are causally ordered in close_mission().
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: boundary_external
symbols.semantics: The Gödel boundary (∂G) implies external authority (⊥_ext) is required. Grounded in lean/SYSTEM.yaml godel_boundary — ∂G is where internal proof stops.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: living_spec_external
symbols.semantics: The living specification (ℒ_FSL) requires external countersign. It cannot prove its own consistency from within — least fixed point of governed evolution.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: ext_blocks_pass
symbols.semantics: External authority at the boundary means the mission cannot autonomously pass internally. The internal gate chain is insufficient at ∂G.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: acknowledged_orphan_set
symbols.semantics: The acknowledged orphan set — missions formally recognized as lifecycle-incomplete at the Gödelian boundary repair of 2026-05-25. Membership in Ack_∅ is an axiom: true-but-unprovable lifecycle statements that enter the system at the boundary rather than through the normal open→close→countersign proof chain. Modeled after Gödel's move — unprovable-within-the-system truths are axiomatized at the boundary.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: derivation_boundary_anchored
symbols.semantics: The derivation relationship (↦_FSL) implies the Gödel boundary (∂G). Any implementation-to-FSL derivation is boundary-anchored — it depends on external chain state that the internal system cannot verify. Proved as derivation_implies_boundary in governance_theorems.lean.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: delta_boundary_anchored
symbols.semantics: The FSL delta (Δ_FSL) implies the Gödel boundary (∂G). The symbol diff between two StateProofs is boundary-anchored — its content is external to the internal system and cannot be internally verified. Proved as part of sync_chain_sound in governance_theorems.lean.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: sync_produces_delta
symbols.semantics: The FSL synchronization event (⊕_sync) produces a symbol delta (Δ_FSL). Sync is the complete FSL write cycle triggered at mission close — it computes the diff between the pre- and post-mission living specification and writes it to fsl/SYSTEM.yaml.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: constitutional_surface_immutable
symbols.semantics: constitution/SYSTEM.yaml modified without ConstitutionalAmendmentPacket → bottom.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: constitutional_lipschitz_bounded
symbols.semantics: Constitutional missions must satisfy Lipschitz bound. Violation requires Ack_∅.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: surface_registry_consistent
symbols.semantics: open_guard._TRUTH_SURFACES must exactly match constitution/SYSTEM.yaml canonical_surfaces. Drift is R4 violation → bottom.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.

symbols.name: floor_bound
symbols.semantics: The coherence floor for any mission is bounded below by FSL_FLOOR_MINIMUM (= 5/6). Any edit to coherence_engine.py that weakens the floor formula violates this invariant. Guards the self-protecting property of the floor constant discovered in MISSION-FSL-P3S-FLOOR-EMPIRICAL.

symbols.name: total_ticks_advance

glyph_candidate.rationale: Tick advance as additive time progression.

symbols.semantics: total_ticks is a bijection from calendar state to a single non-negative integer. Advancing by n ticks adds exactly n to the total.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No active symbol is bound; a future UpdatePacket may promote this candidate.

symbols.name: advance_homomorphism

glyph_candidate.rationale: Time advance as composition-preserving structure.

symbols.semantics: advance is a homomorphism from (ℕ,+) to (TimeState, compose). Path independence — calendar state is fully determined by total tick count. Theorem 1. Proved on real format_time code, not a Lean model.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No active symbol is bound; a future UpdatePacket may promote this candidate.

symbols.name: monoid_action_identity

glyph_candidate.rationale: Time action identity element without changing active theorem naming.

symbols.semantics: advance(s,0) = s for all canonical TimeStates. Identity element. Together with advance_homomorphism: ℕ acts on canonical TimeStates as a monoid. Theorem 2.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No active symbol is bound; a future UpdatePacket may promote this candidate.

symbols.name: governed_bounded_observer

glyph_candidate.rationale: Observer theorem marker with retained GBO mnemonic during transition.

symbols.semantics: U1 top-level conjunction: state decomposition ∧ tick advances ∧ no self-loop ∧ coordination cost positive ∧ zero coordination cost impossible. Formally proved.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: admissible_transition

glyph_candidate.rationale: Admissible observer transition as checked motion.

symbols.semantics: admissible g₁ g₂: DID preserved ∧ tick strictly increases ∧ valid spatial motion. Core admissibility predicate. ¬admissible g g proved by gbo_ii_no_self_loop.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: valid_history

glyph_candidate.rationale: Structured history trace with explicit H mnemonic.

symbols.semantics: Inductive: [] and [g] valid; g₁::g₂::rest valid iff admissible g₁ g₂ and g₂::rest valid. Grounds the directed acyclic structure of GBC state histories.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: no_self_loop

glyph_candidate.rationale: No admissible self-transition or loop.

symbols.semantics: ∀ g, ¬ admissible g g. No state is admissibly reachable from itself.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: coordination_cost_positive

glyph_candidate.rationale: Coordination cost is strictly positive.

symbols.semantics: ∀ p₁ p₂ > 0, lcm(p₁,p₂) > 0. Coordination cost is always strictly positive.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: coordination_cost_ge_max

glyph_candidate.rationale: Coordination cost lower-bound relative to unilateral maximum.

symbols.semantics: ∀ p₁ p₂ > 0, max(p₁,p₂) ≤ lcm(p₁,p₂). Coordination never cheaper than unilateral.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: underdetermination

glyph_candidate.rationale: Multiple networks can present indistinguishable observer views.

symbols.semantics: ∃ N₁ ≠ N₂ with identical GCD view from anchor 0. Witness: N₁ = const 6, N₂ = (if n=0 then 6 else 12). Proves self-certification without anchor is impossible.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: anchor_nonuniqueness

glyph_candidate.rationale: Anchor locus with nonuniqueness mnemonic.

symbols.semantics: ∃ view ∈ AchievableView, ∃ N₁ ≠ N₂ both realizing view from anchor 0. Existential anchor irreducibility over achievable views. Fully constructive.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: achievable_view

glyph_candidate.rationale: Achievable modal view with retained V mnemonic.

symbols.semantics: AchievableView view ≡ ∃ N a, ∀ i, gcd(N a, N i) = view i. Restricts anchor reasoning to views realizable as GCD patterns; prevents false universalization over all ℕ → ℕ functions.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: imp_zero_cost

glyph_candidate.rationale: Zero-cost coordination is impossible.

symbols.semantics: ¬∃ p₁ p₂ > 0, lcm(p₁,p₂) = 0. Zero coordination cost is formally impossible.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: imp_self_certification

glyph_candidate.rationale: Self-certification without external anchor is impossible.

symbols.semantics: Self-certification without anchor is impossible — underdetermination holds.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: spatial_horizon

glyph_candidate.rationale: Spatial observer horizon shorthand.

symbols.semantics: Every observer has a spatial horizon — dark matter beyond the visible cell.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: temporal_horizon

glyph_candidate.rationale: Temporal observer horizon shorthand.

symbols.semantics: Every observer has a temporal horizon — tick lag bounded by refresh period.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: valid_spatial_motion

glyph_candidate.rationale: Spatial motion admitted by observer law.

symbols.semantics: Motion is valid iff stay | zoom_in | zoom_out | is_neighbor. Axiomatized over the HTM lattice. Ground truth is HTMCell.is_neighbor.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: face_adjacency

glyph_candidate.rationale: Symmetric adjacency relation between HTM root faces.

symbols.semantics: Cube graph Q₃ on Fin 8: two root faces adjacent iff 8-bit indices differ by one bit. Symmetric (face_adj_symm) and irreflexive (face_adj_irrefl). 12 edges total.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: face_adj_geometric

glyph_candidate.rationale: Triangular surface face geometry with retained F mnemonic.

symbols.semantics: face_adj = faces_share_edge (two octahedron faces share a geometric edge). Proved native_decide over all 64 root face pairs. Connects Q₃ graph to octahedron.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: inner_sibling_geometry

glyph_candidate.rationale: Trixel sibling geometry within one subdivided face.

symbols.semantics: In the 4-trixel HTM subdivision, center (child 0) shares a geometric edge with each corner child; corner-corner pairs share only one vertex (no edge). Proved decide over all 16 child pairs (Fin 4 × Fin 4).
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: depth1_geometry

glyph_candidate.rationale: Exact depth-1 triangular adjacency geometry.

symbols.semantics: d1_adj = inner_sib_bool ∨ cross_adj. The depth-1 geometric shared-edge relation equals inner-sibling OR precise cross-parent (adjacent faces, both corners, same shared octahedron vertex). Proved native_decide over 1024 pairs (8×4×8×4). Exact at depth 1.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: htm_neighbor

glyph_candidate.rationale: HTM neighbor relation as bidirectional adjacency.

symbols.semantics: Refined HTMCell.is_neighbor: inner-sibling (center↔corner, same face, any depth) ∨ cross-parent (adjacent faces, both corners, same shared octahedron vertex via caxis). Exact characterization at depth 1 by d1_adj_correct. Symmetric and irreflexive.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: neighbor_symmetric

glyph_candidate.rationale: Neighbor symmetry as reversible adjacency.

symbols.semantics: is_neighbor c₁ c₂ → is_neighbor c₂ c₁. Spatial adjacency is undirected.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: neighbor_irreflexive

glyph_candidate.rationale: No cell is its own neighbor, preserving the neighbor mnemonic.

symbols.semantics: ¬ is_neighbor c c. No cell is spatially adjacent to itself.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: neighbor_depth_preserved

glyph_candidate.rationale: Neighbor relation preserves HTM depth.

symbols.semantics: is_neighbor c₁ c₂ → c₁.depth = c₂.depth. Adjacency is depth-homogeneous.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: Active symbol or theorem references require a compatibility alias plan before promotion.

symbols.name: sha256_binding
symbols.semantics: SHA-256-based commitment is computationally binding: no efficient algorithm can find distinct g₁ ≠ g₂ with commit g₁ = commit g₂. Cryptographic assumption — unprovable internally. Replaces the false gbo_vi_commitment_injective (injectivity is false by pigeonhole). Requires bundle(simon.crypto.v1) active in scope.
symbols.promotion_gate_reason: No glyph candidate is proposed for this symbol.
